mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns. This is a persistent, always-on signal of underlying economic strength.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a leading indicator of recession, triggering investor risk aversion and negative equity returns as future earnings estimates are revised downwards.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a valid trigger event.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it signals the central bank is 'behind the curve'. This implies future aggressive tightening, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: The two-factor model fails to show a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.05) on the interaction term over a 5-year lookback.

mechanism_narrative: During an established monetary tightening cycle, each subsequent increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) further increases the discount rate on future earnings and signals the central bank's commitment to slowing the economy, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The beta of S&P 500 returns to FEDFUNDS changes is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) during periods identified as tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS, adjusted for CPIAUCSL) indicates an abrupt weakening of consumer demand, which has a rapid negative impact on investor sentiment and near-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a healthy manufacturing and supply side of the economy, supporting corporate revenue growth and leading to positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: A strategy going long S&P 500 when INDPRO 3-month change is positive and short when negative fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant year-over-year contraction in housing starts (HOUST) serves as a strong leading indicator of a broad-based economic slowdown, given housing's sensitivity to interest rates and its multiplier effect, thus predicting negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following a trigger, the S&P 500 posts a positive return in more than 50% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and high inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals an overheating economy, forcing the central bank into an aggressive tightening policy that is detrimental to equity valuations.
failure_condition: A portfolio that is short the S&P 500 only when the trigger condition is met fails to produce a statistically significant positive alpha over a full business cycle.
